title: Don't try to resolve host path if copying from stdin
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

Resolves #25472

Calling `copy.ResolveHostPath` fails when copying from stdin on Windows as /dev/stdin isn't a real folder. The result of `copy.ResolveHostPath` isn't actually used in the stdin code flow, so an easy fix is to condition the call on whether isStdin is true or false. The only potential behavior change is that, if for some unlikely reason /dev/stdin was missing on Linux or MacOS, the copy command would fail with a different error than it did previously.

comments.body: This scenario is [already covered in the existing tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/919247a77cbea0b0d5edab573b287ca481f5b700/test/system/065-cp.bats#L863); there's not really any additional test coverage to add outside of the general ability to run the tests on Windows.

> This scenario is [already covered in the existing tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/919247a77cbea0b0d5edab573b287ca481f5b700/test/system/065-cp.bats#L863); there's not really any additional test coverage to add outside of the general ability to run the tests on Windows.

Right. Machine tests are the ones that are run on Windows, and you can find them in [pkg/machine/e2e/](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/machine/e2e/README.md)
I'm updating the e2e tests to include copy over stdin.

Thank you @danegsta for submitting this PR and in particular for adding the first cp e2e test for machine. Please address @Luap99 suggestions and remember to sign your commit so that [DCO check passes](https://probot.github.io/apps/dco/).
I've squashed the commits and made sure to sign. Sorry for the extra commit noise as this is my first PR to the project, so needed to get my head wrapped around the expected commit format.
LGTM, I have retriggered the tests that are failing as failures don't seem related to this PR.

Sorry for just mention this now but can you fix up the commit message.
```
Add e2e test for podman cp using stdio

Fix lint error

Simplify resetting stdin

Update new test case, avoid additional if check

Make test stdio a Reader

Fix lint errors
```

There is no useful information in this so just drop it. And instead `Fixes: #25472` should be added in the commit message not just the PR description, https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#describe-your-changes-in-commit-messages

LGTM, ready to merge once the commit message is updated.
/approve
I've amended the commit message to remove the messages from the individual squashed commits (and added the fixed issue reference).

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *danegsta*, *Luap99*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99,mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
